calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Adminstrative Writ
Off Calendar Mar-28-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Fleer, Esq., 925.930.9518, for Petitioner Patricia Weiss.
Ashley Harlan, Deputy Attorney General, 415.903.5709, for respondent Board of Psychology, State of California.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: PETITIONER PATRICIA WEISS' Petition For Adminstrative Writ is granted. Although Dr. Weiss has not established that the administrative finding that she violated Bus. & Prof. Code secs. 2960(n) and (p) is contrary to the weight of the evidence, Dr. Weiss has established that the evidence does not support the other administrative findings. As to Bus. & Prof. Code sec. 2960(k), the evidence does not support the finding that Dr. Weiss violated Ethical Standards 3.10, 3.07, and 9.01. Dr. Weiss had a good faith belief that the study was Court approved based on the email from attorney Kristina Kliszewski stating that the Court approved funding. The evidence is ambiguous as to whether Dr. Weiss attempted to contact the social worker. Additionally, there is diametrically different expert testimony as to whether Dr. Weiss' observations were sufficient to substantiate her findings. Further, Dr. Weiss has established that she may not be subject to discipline for violating Ethical Principle C, as the American Psychological Association Guidelines explicitly state that "General Principles, in contrast to Ethical Standards, do not represent obligations and should not form the basis for imposing sanctions." As to Bus. & Prof. Code sec. 2960(j), the evidence does not support a finding that Dr. Weiss' conduct was "an extreme departure from the ordinary standard of conduct or the lack of even scant care." (Kearl v. Board of Medical Quality Assurance (1986) 189 Cal.App.3d 1040, 1052.) Dr. Weiss has established that the imposition of the maximum penalty, revocation of her license, was a manifest abuse of discretion. (Landau v. Medical Board (1988) 81 Cal.App.4th 191, 218.) The evidence establishes that Dr. Weiss has never been the subject of other disciplinary proceedings, Dr. Weiss will not perform another bonding study in the future, and Dr. Weiss acted on a good faith belief that there was a court order to perform the bonding study. Further, no evidence was presented that even if the bonding study would have been admitted into evidence by the court, harm would have come to the children. (See generally Magit v. Board of Medical Examiners (1961) 57 Cal.2d 74.) The matter is remanded to the Board of Psychology to set aside its order of revocation and impose appropriate discipline consistent with this order. The discipline imposed shall not exceed probation. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Deborah Neville 703.

Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Adminstrative Writ
Off Calendar Mar-26-2014 Continued To Mar-28-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Adminstrative Writ
Off Calendar Mar-21-2014 Continued To Mar-26-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONER PATRICIA WEISS' Hearing On Petition For Adminstrative Writ is continued to March 26, 2014, on the Court's motion. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976

Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Adminstrative Writ
Off Calendar Mar-05-2014 Continued To Mar-21-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
John L. Fleer, Esq. of the Law Offices of John L. Fleer, Tel. 925.930.9518, for petitioner and moving party;
Ashley Harlan, Esq., Deputy Attorney General, Tel. 415.708.6709, for respondent and opposing party.

RULING: Petitioner's counsel brought before the Court a request to extend the stay of license revocation pending the writ proceeding and to change the continued hearing date to March 21, 2014. Counsel for respondent objected to the extension of stay but agreed to the new hearing date. The matter was discussed. After hearing, the Court adopted its tentative ruling as modified: PETITIONER PATRICIA WEISS' Hearing On Petition For Adminstrative Writ is continued on the Court's own motion to March 21, 2014 at 9:30 am in Department 302. Order to extend the stay was signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.
